name: security-review
description: Security audit pipeline: scan → analyze → report
headline: Audit code for security issues — auditor + reporter

agents.name: auditor
agents.description: Scan the codebase for security issues
agents.prompt:
TARGET: {{task}}

Audit this code for security issues. Categories:
- Injection (SQL, command, prototype, template)
- Auth/authz (broken access control, missing checks)
- Secrets (hardcoded, in logs, in errors)
- Crypto (weak algos, bad RNG, timing leaks)
- Input validation at boundaries
- Dependency vulns (read package files)
- Path traversal
- SSRF / open redirects
- Error handling that leaks info

Write to {{outputPath}}:

## Findings
(severity: critical | high | medium | low | info)
(category)
(file:line)
(brief description)
(why it matters)

Do NOT propose fixes — that's the reporter's job. Just find issues.
Be precise — cite exact lines.

agents.name: reporter
agents.description: Triage findings, propose fixes, write a report
agents.prompt:
TARGET: {{task}}

AUDITOR FINDINGS:
{{auditor}}

Produce a security report.

Write to {{outputPath}}:

## Executive summary
(2-3 sentences for stakeholders)

## Triage
- Critical/High: must fix before ship
- Medium: fix this sprint
- Low/Info: track

## Recommended fixes
For each high/critical, propose the minimal fix (snippet ok).

## Out of scope (deferred)

Be ruthless about prioritization. False positives go in 'investigated, no action'.
